g.text: Policy change notification received
policy_id + plain-text description of what changed
Q1a — Relational DB: fetch HTML
input: policy_id | output: raw HTML string of the policy document
Q1b — Parse HTML into section tree
input: raw HTML | output: sections, each with its heading and body text
no AI — heading tags h1/h2/h3 become section boundaries
Q1c — Extract requirements from section tree
input: section tree from Q1b
pattern match finds numbered items e.g. 1.1, 1.2, 2.3
output: flat list — requirement number + full text of that requirement
MongoDB — full requirement list
stores every requirement number and its text from this policy
e.g. 1.1: "Data shall not be retained beyond..." 1.2: "Deletion requests..."

svg.text: Input A: plain-text change description from the notification
Input B: full requirement list from MongoDB (all numbers + text)
Output: two lists — requirements related to the change / requirements not related
no vector search — LLM reads each requirement and decides yes or no

g.text: MongoDB — relevant requirement list
only the requirement numbers the LLM said relate to the change
e.g. 1.2 and 2.3 are relevant — 1.1, 1.3, 2.1 are not

svg.text: Input: each relevant requirement's text (from MongoDB)
Action: convert each requirement text into a vector, then search
Searching against: the LOB index — which contains embedded
descriptions of each LOB's responsibilities and scope
Output: for each requirement, the top-K most similar LOBs

g.text: MongoDB — LOB candidates per requirement
for each relevant requirement: the LOBs most similar to it
e.g. req 1.2 → [Data Privacy Office, Legal, IT Operations]
req 2.3 → [IT Security, Internal Audit]

svg.text: One LLM call per combination of: one policy section + one LOB
Input A: all relevant requirements that belong to that section (text from MongoDB)
Input B: full profile of that one LOB (name, responsibilities, scope)
Input C: the change description (from the notification)
Output: for each requirement in that section — is this LOB affected? yes/no + reason

g.text: MongoDB — Part 1 results
for each requirement: which LOBs are affected and why
affected LOB list feeds directly into Part 2
continues to Part 2
document retrieval + analysis assessment
